student_message: Is it S? I always get it wrong. This is too hard.
final_message:
Math can definitely feel hard sometimes, Riya. Take a big, deep breath with me! I am so proud of how you used your fingers to figure things out earlier. Making mistakes is just how our brains grow, and I believe in you! 💛 

Let's look at the letter $$S$$ together. If we draw a straight line right down the middle and fold the paper, do the two sides match perfectly? 

To help us remember what that special fold is called, try this fun riddle. If you fold a perfect butterfly on my back, its wings match exactly! I make sure the left side is a perfect mirror twin of the right side. What kind of magical math line am I? ✨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Comfort Riya, regain her focus, and gently help her check if S has a fold line.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: Riya is sad and highly distracted. She needs encouragement and engagement to rebuild confidence and focus before addressing her guess.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
session_summary: The session covered symmetry and symmetrical letters. Riya visually understands symmetry, successfully recognizing matching parts and "fold lines." However, she struggles to identify symmetrical letters (guessing R and P) and recall formal vocabulary. Her current learning state is highly frustrated and overwhelmed. Exhibiting negative self-talk, she repeatedly states the material is "too hard." She lacks confidence and requires constant encouragement to connect her accurate visual understanding to the correct mathematical terms.
